channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Near 1 Allington Road, 23rd April
item.description:
Flytipping

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kilburn Lane, North Kensington, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Medium - Up to medium size van load (Estates)

item.title: Complaint Regarding Illegal Dumping Outside My Property, 23rd April
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to raise a formal complaint regarding ongoing illegal dumping outside my property. This is now the second time a large item (a fridge) has been left directly in front of my home, which is located on a corner road. This situation is causing me significant anxiety and distress, especially as people already leave cans and takeaway waste around my property. Following this latest incident, I carried out my own enquiries and spoke with nearby residents. I have reason to believe the item originates from property number 262. The fridge freezer box was left directly in front of that property’s window, where there are also chairs and a bench. In addition, the box packaging from a new fridge is currently left under the window of number 262, further may indicating a connection to the dumped appliance. This is not the case for number 264. I kindly request that this matter is investigated as soon as possible, and if feasible, that an officer attends before the next bin collection to verify the situation before any evidence is removed. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Yours faithfully, Faila Lumande Mottingham road SE9 4SZ

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mottingham Road, Coldharbour, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Found SD0, 1 & 2 all faulty/pd found no comms to 1st RP, suspect line of sight issue due to large amounts of foliage, see pic for ref., 23rd April
item.description:
Found SD0, 1 & 2 all faulty/pd found no comms to 1st RP, suspect line of sight issue due to large amounts of foliage, see pic for ref. Cut required by borough

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): High Road, Fallow Corner, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Significant broken glass in public walkway/alleyway, 23rd April
item.description:
The alleyway behind St Christopher’s Inn pub is becoming increasingly covered in broken glass. This is a well-used pedestrian route for staff and students of King’s College London travelling between Borough High Street and the main campus, as well as for Premier Inn guests and other members of the public. The area is also frequently used by people sitting on the benches to drink alcohol and loiter, although it is not clear whether the broken glass is coming from pub-related activity or from members of the public using the space. Regardless of its source, the quantity of broken glass is now significant. This creates both a clear safety risk for pedestrians and an increasingly poor street environment. I would be grateful if the council could arrange for the area to be cleared and consider what further measures may be needed to prevent the problem from recurring.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18–20, Newcomen Street, The Borough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Covering Drive, 23rd April
item.description:
This van has constantly parked and covered the driveway. As usual, cars are parking there and not only blocking access to the drive, but also making it very hard for cars to pass by. I witnessed a lorry not being able to pass today because of this vehicles parking.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Commonwealth Way, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: No cleaner, 23rd April
item.description:
Streets are not being cleaned just like the leaves weren’t now the tree is letting of some what substance that causes allergies I’m cleaning it all the time I really don’t know what the service charge is being used for because it’s not a cleaner

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Richmond Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
